metadata.role_title: AI Engineer - Machine Learning (Max.AI team, 1-3 yrs)
metadata.recent_signal: U.S. patent granted for ZS Atlas Intelligence core capability (May 2026); Tech Innovation Lab paper accepted at PMSA 2026 Annual conference
metadata.match_reason: Dedicated Max.AI engineering unit building GenAI products; Job ID 32712 Bengaluru 1-3 yrs ML; Python + MLOps (MLflow) + production ML pipelines; PySpark; Airflow; SageMaker/Kubeflow; AWS/Azure/GCP
metadata.cold_outreach_angle: ZS Atlas Intelligence patent (May 2026) + Max.AI Platforms and Products unit; Anirban Das team's AI Factory for Agentic Solutions paper at PMSA 2026; Amit's RAG/MLOps/PySpark background maps directly to JD
metadata.notes: 127 ZS Associates jobs on Naukri; large GenAI practice across pharma/healthcare; email pattern first.last@zs.com (85%)

research_summary: ZS (formerly ZS Associates) is a $2.7B management consulting + technology firm for life sciences/pharma/healthcare with 10K+ employees. Their Max.AI / Platforms and Products team (Req ID 32712, Bengaluru) builds ML engineering platforms using Python, PySpark, MLflow, Airflow, SageMaker, Kubeflow, and cloud. ZS just received a U.S. patent for ZS Atlas Intelligence (May 2026). Their Tech Innovation Lab published an AI Factory for Enterprise Agentic Solutions paper at PMSA 2026. Email pattern: first.last@zs.com. 8 contacts captured: 3 Tier 1 (senior ML), 3 Tier 3 (junior/mid ML), 2 Tier 4 (recruiters).
jd_full_text:
AI Engineer - Machine Learning | Req ID: 32712 | Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India | Category: Platforms and Products

ZS is a place where passion changes lives. As a management consulting and technology firm focused on improving life and how we live it, we transform ideas into impact by bringing together data, science, technology and human ingenuity to deliver better outcomes for all. Here you'll work side-by-side with a powerful collective of thinkers and experts shaping life-changing solutions for patients, caregivers and consumers, worldwide. ZSers drive impact by bringing a client-first mentality to each and every engagement. We partner collaboratively with our clients to develop custom solutions and technology products that create value and deliver company results across critical areas of their business. Bring your curiosity for learning, bold ideas, courage and passion to drive life-changing impact to ZS.

What You'll Do:
Build, Refine and Use ML Engineering platforms and components.
Scaling machine learning algorithms to work on massive data sets and strict SLAs.
Build and orchestrate model pipelines including feature engineering, inferencing and continuous model training.
Implement ML Ops including model KPI measurements, tracking, model drift & model feedback loop.
Collaborate with client facing teams to understand business context at a high level and contribute in technical requirement gathering.
Implement basic features aligning with technical requirements.
Write production-ready code that is easily testable, understood by other developers and accounts for edge cases and errors.
Ensure highest quality of deliverables by following architecture/design guidelines, coding best practices, periodic design/code reviews.
Write unit tests as well as higher level tests to handle expected edge cases and errors gracefully, as well as happy paths.
Uses bug tracking, code review, version control and other tools to organize and deliver work.
Participate in scrum calls and agile ceremonies, and effectively communicate work progress, issues and dependencies.
Consistently contribute in researching & evaluating latest architecture patterns/technologies through rapid learning, conducting proof-of-concepts and creating prototype solutions.

What You'll Bring:
A master's or bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field from a top university.
1-3 years' hands-on experience in ML development.
Good understanding of the fundamentals of machine learning.
Strong programming expertise in Python, PySpark/Scala.
Expertise in crafting ML Models for high performance and scalability.
Experience in implementing feature engineering, inferencing pipelines, and real time model predictions.
Experience in ML Ops to measure and track model performance, experience working with MLFlow.
Experience with Spark or other distributed computing frameworks.
Experience in ML platforms like Sage maker, Kubeflow.
Experience with pipeline orchestration tools such Airflow.
Experience in deploying models to cloud services like AWS, Azure, GCP, Azure ML.
Expertise in SQL, SQL DB's.
Knowledgeable of core CS concepts such as common data structures and algorithms.
Collaborate well with teams with different backgrounds / expertise / functions.
Fluency in English.
Client-first mentality.
Intense work ethic.
Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach.
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with ML engineering platforms and inferencing pipelines mirroring ZS JD language; quantified MRR improvement (45% to 95%) frames MLOps track record. Skills reordered: Languages/Frameworks first (Python/SQL prominent per JD), then ML Engineering/MLOps category, then Cloud/AWS, then LLMs, then Vector DBs; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi reduced to 1 bullet (1,000+ model config evaluation maps to ML fundamentals in JD). Projects: Voicer swapped for On-Premise OCR + Document Entity Extraction (inferencing pipelines, zero-cloud data work maps to ZS MLOps JD); WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot (NLP/Text-to-SQL production system adds new signal vs work experience bullets).

contacts.contact_role: Senior AI Engineer, Tech Innovation Lab | LLMOps | Gen AI | NLP
contacts.research_hook: His team published 'AI Factory Approach for Enterprise Agentic Solutions' paper accepted at PMSA 2026 Annual conference (1 month ago). He works in ZS's Tech Innovation Lab building LLMOps/GenAI systems for pharma clients.
contacts.subject: AI Factory paper at PMSA 2026
contacts.content:
Hi Anirban,

Saw the PMSA 2026 acceptance for the AI Factory approach your team built. Getting enterprise agentic work into a pharma conference is a different kind of validation.

Quick context on why I'm writing: I'm 2 years into production ML at Vivansh Infotech. Built permission-aware agentic RAG (LangChain/LangGraph) serving 500+ concurrent enterprise users with 250+ permissions enforced per query, and multi-model inference pipelines with GPT-4o, Qwen3-Coder, and Gemini. Before that, Silver Touch: rebuilt a 50K-queries/month retrieval stack from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models; MRR from 45% to 95%.

I came across the AI Engineer (ML) role at ZS (Req ID 32712) and the fit looks real. Python, MLOps, inferencing pipelines, production-ready code at scale.

If you have 15 minutes, I'd be curious how the Tech Innovation Lab approaches model drift and feedback loops at ZS's client scale. That context would help me decide whether to apply formally.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: Re: AI Factory paper at PMSA 2026
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Anirban,

Following up in case the first note got buried.

One thing I've been thinking about since I sent it: the factory metaphor for enterprise agentic AI is interesting specifically because it implies repeatable pipelines with measurable outputs. Most of my production work has been one-off systems that turn into informal platforms. The deliberate architecture piece is what I want to learn more about.

If the timing doesn't work, totally fine. Happy to connect when you're next looking at ML hires.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: Re: AI Factory paper at PMSA 2026
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Anirban,

Last note from me. If ZS isn't hiring on ML right now or this isn't the right fit, no worries at all.

Either way, good luck with the PMSA presentation.

Amit
amitgohel.dev

contacts.research_hook: Senior AI Engineer at ZS. ZS just received a U.S. patent for ZS Atlas Intelligence (May 2026) -- relevant hook for the Max.AI / Platforms and Products team where this role sits.
contacts.subject: ZS Atlas Intelligence patent + ML engineer question
contacts.content:
Hi Harshit,

Noticed ZS just got a U.S. patent for a core capability in Atlas Intelligence. That kind of investment in proprietary analytics infrastructure is what drew me to the Platforms and Products team.

I'm writing because the AI Engineer (ML) role (Req ID 32712) is a close match for where I am. Two years building production ML pipelines: rebuilt a retrieval platform handling 50K+ queries/month, improved MRR from 45% to 95% through embedding model benchmarking (tested 6 models on production data), cut inference latency from 16s to 8s. Current role is agentic AI at scale using Python, LangChain/LangGraph, and AWS.

One question: how does the ML engineering team at ZS think about the tradeoff between model standardization and per-client customization, given the pharma client base? That tension comes up in every production ML system I've worked on and I'm curious how ZS handles it at scale.

Happy to share a resume or have a short call.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: Re: ZS Atlas Intelligence patent + ML engineer question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Harshit,

Bumping this up in case it got lost.

Since sending the first note I also looked at how ZS describes the Platforms and Products work publicly. The description of Atlas Intelligence as a platform for consumer insights maps pretty directly to the kind of inferencing pipeline work I've been doing. The scale difference is interesting though -- pharma client data is structured very differently from what I've been working with.

If you're not hiring on ML right now, no problem. Happy to check back in a quarter.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: Re: ZS Atlas Intelligence patent + ML engineer question
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Harshit,

Last one from me. If this role is filled or the timing doesn't work, I completely understand.

Would still appreciate being on your radar for future ML openings at ZS.

Amit
amitgohel.dev

contacts.research_hook: Posts about 'Scaling AI Products | GenAI, ML, Graphs | 40+ Clients | LLMOps | Decision Intelligence'. Shared ZS NLP job openings in Pune. Advocates for LLMOps and decision intelligence at scale across 40+ clients -- aligns with Amit's multi-model orchestration and RAG work.
contacts.subject: Decision intelligence at 40+ clients, question
contacts.content:
Hi Viresh,

Your framing around decision intelligence at ZS caught my attention. Building that across 40+ pharma clients means standardizing ML decision layers for very different data environments. That's a harder problem than most ML infra work.

Background: two years on production ML pipelines. Rebuilt a 50K-queries/month retrieval system at Silver Touch (from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data, MRR from 45% to 95%). Now building multi-model agentic AI at Vivansh handling 500+ concurrent enterprise users. The MLOps side -- model performance tracking, latency optimization, feedback loops -- is where most of the interesting problems lived.

ZS's AI Engineer (ML) role in Bengaluru (Req ID 32712) is what I'm looking at. The fit on Python, MLOps, and inferencing pipelines looks clean.

If you're open to it, I'd be curious what the most common ML deployment failure mode looks like across ZS's client portfolio. Even 10 minutes would be useful.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: Re: Decision intelligence at 40+ clients, question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Viresh,

Following up on this in case my first note got buried.

One thing I've been thinking about since: the challenge of decision intelligence at pharma scale is that the ground truth for "correct" recommendations changes as regulatory context changes. That's a model drift problem with external triggers, not just data drift. I don't know how ZS handles it but I'd guess it requires fairly opinionated MLOps pipelines.

If now's not a good time, no problem. Happy to reconnect when things open up on the ML side.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: Re: Decision intelligence at 40+ clients, question
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Viresh,

Last note from me. If ZS isn't actively hiring ML right now or this isn't the right fit, I understand.

Good luck with the LLMOps work -- the problems you're describing publicly are genuinely interesting.

Amit
amitgohel.dev

contacts.contact_role: Senior Software Engineer, AI & LLM Systems | PySpark | AWS

contacts.subject: PySpark + LLM systems at ZS, quick question
contacts.content:
Hi Mohak,

Your title caught my attention -- AI and LLM systems with PySpark and AWS is exactly the stack called out in ZS's AI Engineer (ML) role (Req ID 32712).

I'm two years into production ML: rebuilt a 50K-queries/month retrieval pipeline at Silver Touch (FAISS to Milvus, MRR 45% to 95%), and now building agentic AI at Vivansh handling 500+ concurrent enterprise users. Strong on Python, AWS, and multi-model orchestration. PySpark is something I've worked with at smaller scale and I'm actively building depth there.

One practical question: what does the day-to-day ML engineering work look like at ZS? The JD is clear on tech stack but less clear on how the platform work fits into the client delivery model.

If you have 10 minutes sometime, I'd appreciate the context.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
